calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-24-2010 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing As To Defendant Mark De Jong, Only.
Off Calendar Aug-12-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr., Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Marc D. Coopersmith, Esq. appeared for defendant Golden State Lumber, Inc., a California corporation, also doing business as Sierra Point Lumber.
Danny Della-Santina, general manager for plaintiff, was place under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Mark De Jong, a/k/a Mark De Long, an individual.
Default was entered against defendant Mark De Jong, a/k/a Mark De Long, an individual, on April 29, 2010.
Bankruptcy stay as to defendant Bloemendal Construction Company, Inc., a California corporation.
Defendants Christopher Roque Versace, Karine A. Versace, Pamela A. Barry, Douglas A. Barry, Mortgage Electronic Registration Systems, Inc., First American Title Company, USAA Federal Savings Bank, Juan Echartea, Suretec Insurance Company and Bode Concrete LLC have all been dismissed from the case.
Doe defendants were dismissed on May 7, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Golden State Lumber, Inc., a California corporation, also doing business as Sierra Point Lumber, and against the Defaulted Defendant Mark De Jong, a/k/a Mark De Long, an individual, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $15,528.16.
Prejudgment Interest - $686.61.
Attorney's Fees - $2,825.00.
Costs - Waived.
Total - $19,039.77.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2010 Continued To Dec-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-14-2010 Set For Order To Show Cause On Aug-09-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
